title: Product Manager, Relevance and Personalization
score_rationale: Riley's experience is a near-textbook match for this role: hybrid AI search, search ranking, personalization/recommendation systems, A/B experimentation, and cross-functional partnership with ML engineers and researchers are all core to their Samsung and Kolon work. The domain alignment is excellent — Airbnb's R&P team sits at the intersection of search ranking, recommendations, personalization, and generative AI, which mirrors Riley's stated target archetypes and lived experience. The only meaningful gap is years of experience: the role calls for 10+ years and Riley has 9, which is a minor stretch, and the seniority signals (owning strategy at global scale, multi-stakeholder alignment across Guest/Host/MarTech/Trust) lean toward a Lead or Staff PM level — one step above Riley's target — making this a competitive but realistic stretch application.
recommendation_reason: Domain, archetype, and skills are near-perfect matches; the 10+ year requirement is a minor gap Riley can credibly address with the depth of their impact.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience shipping search ranking, personalization, and recommendation systems at scale — including hybrid AI search serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation engines on a $2B+ GMV platform. Expert at partnering with ML engineers and applied researchers to define relevance strategy, design rigorous A/B experiments, and translate complex technical tradeoffs into measurable business outcomes across global consumer marketplaces.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers
gap_handling: The JD asks for 10+ years; Riley has 9. Frame it confidently — don't volunteer the gap. The depth of impact (10M+ users, 3 international markets, $2B+ GMV, measurable ML metric improvements) speaks louder than a 12-month delta. In cover materials, lead with scope and scale, not tenure. Additionally, Airbnb's "Tripcycle" vision and marketplace health/fairness objectives (host success, trust) are nuances worth addressing — briefly reference cross-marketplace or two-sided platform thinking from the Amazon B2B seller experience.

title: Product Manager, Guest Discovery and Homepage
location: San Francisco CA, Los Angeles CA, New York NY
score_rationale: Riley's core competencies — AI-powered discovery, search ranking, personalization, recommendation systems, and A/B experimentation at scale — map almost perfectly to what Airbnb is building on its homepage. The Samsung experience (10M+ monthly users, hybrid search, conversational AI, product discovery funnels) and the Kolon e-commerce background (recommendation systems, CTR optimization, conversion funnel) provide direct, quantified evidence for this exact domain. The primary gap is consumer marketplace / travel context (Airbnb's unique supply-demand two-sided marketplace) and a modest stretch on the "product narrative / marketing partnership" axis, which Riley has touched but not headlined.
recommendation_reason: 9 years of AI-powered discovery and search at consumer scale is a near-perfect domain match; strong quantified evidence across every core requirement.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping search, discovery, and personalization experiences at consumer scale — including a hybrid AI search platform serving 10M+ monthly users at Samsung.com and ML-driven recommendation systems on a $2B+ GMV e-commerce platform. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers, data scientists, and UX teams to build AI-powered browsing experiences that measurably lift engagement, conversion, and retention.
top_bullets: Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI — increasing web navigation NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 → $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing on a $2B+ GMV platform.
gap_handling: The JD emphasizes "product narrative / marketing storytelling" and lifecycle campaign partnership — areas Riley has touched (AEO pipeline, conversational AI GTM, CRM marketing) but not headlined. In the resume and cover letter, explicitly frame the AEO pipeline launch (8x AI agent traffic, +40% conversion in 12 weeks) and the conversational AI chat experience as examples of crafting product narrative and driving cross-functional marketing alignment, not just engineering delivery.

title: Senior Product Manager, Shopping Experience
score_rationale: Riley's 9 years of PM experience spanning e-commerce, AI-powered discovery, personalization, and recommendation systems maps extremely well to Instacart's Shopping Experience team. The Samsung and Kolon roles demonstrate repeated ownership of end-to-end consumer discovery funnels with strong, quantified outcomes — exactly the evidence this role demands. The one meaningful gap is the Health/wellness domain specificity; Riley has no explicit nutrition, dietary, or health product background, though the preferred (not required) nature of that qualifier softens the risk considerably.
recommendation_reason: Strong e-commerce discovery + personalization + experimentation background directly satisfies all minimum qualifications; health domain gap is a preferred, not required, criterion.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years building consumer discovery, personalization, and recommendation experiences at Samsung and Amazon that serve millions of users at scale. Proven track record shipping zero-to-one products — from hybrid AI search to ML-driven recommendation systems — with measurable impact on conversion, engagement, and retention in fast-moving e-commerce environments.
top_bullets: Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion.

Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing with data scientists, engineers, and UX designers.

Streamlined product discovery funnels by launching category-specific finder experiences and optimizing taxonomy — increasing PDP view rate per visit by 45% and reducing bounce rate by 50% via improved assortment visibility and guided shopping flows.
gap_handling: Address the health/nutrition domain gap proactively in your cover letter: frame your discovery funnel and personalization expertise as directly transferable (helping users find *the right* products based on filters, signals, and preferences), and note genuine curiosity about health-forward commerce. If you have any personal interest in nutrition, wellness apps, or dietary-conscious shopping, briefly mention it — it signals authentic motivation for the specific product area.

title: Senior Product Manager, AI (Customer Agent)
score_rationale: Riley's profile is a strong match on AI/ML product experience — conversational AI, LLM pipelines, search ranking, and recommendation systems all transfer well to Klaviyo's Customer Agent role. However, the role is squarely B2B SaaS (brand-facing configuration, onboarding flows, and agent tooling for business customers), while Riley's primary archetypes are AI Search and E-commerce Discovery — the B2B platform exposure at Amazon is real but narrower than what this role demands. Riley also lacks explicit RAG/agent architecture experience stated on the resume, and has no direct messaging-channel PM background (SMS, RCS, email automation), which is central to Klaviyo's multi-channel agent vision.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and conversational product credentials clear the bar, but limited B2B SaaS onboarding and multi-channel messaging experience creates a meaningful gap that requires targeted resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered and conversational AI experiences — including an end-to-end on-site AI chat agent, hybrid search systems, and personalized recommendation pipelines serving 10M+ users. Proven track record collaborating with ML/data science teams to define agent behavior, build feedback loops, and drive measurable outcomes across complex B2C and B2B product surfaces.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users
Defined an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80%, partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines
gap_handling: Riley's resume doesn't explicitly mention RAG, agent architectures, or multi-channel deployment (SMS/RCS/email). Before applying, add a brief callout in the conversational AI bullet noting any retrieval or grounding mechanisms used, and consider adding a line about channel orchestration if the Samsung chat experience touched multiple surfaces. If RAG/LLM infrastructure was involved even peripherally, name it explicitly.

title: Staff Product Manager, Search Algorithms
location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
score_rationale: Riley's search algorithm experience at Samsung.com is a strong match — hybrid AI search, relevance ranking, precision@3 metrics, and behavioral signal optimization are directly transferable to Faire's Discovery/Search pillar. However, the role is titled Staff PM and the JD signals direct CEO/founder collaboration and "7+ years at high-growth startup" framing, placing it at a stretch level above Riley's Senior/PM III target. The B2B wholesale marketplace domain (retailer-to-brand discovery) is adjacent but different from B2C e-commerce, and Faire will want someone with startup-scale autonomy rather than large-enterprise PM experience.
recommendation_reason: Excellent search algorithm alignment and strong metric-driven evidence, but Staff-level seniority is a stretch and in-person NYC/SF hybrid requirement is a location hurdle from Bellevue.
suggested_summary: AI Search PM with 9+ years shipping production-grade search and discovery systems — from hybrid ranking and semantic relevance to query understanding and behavioral signal optimization — serving 10M+ monthly users. Proven track record owning search strategy end-to-end alongside engineering, data science, and executive stakeholders, with measurable gains in precision, CTR, and conversion across large-scale e-commerce platforms.
top_bullets: Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%

Led hybrid AI-powered search experience launch across 3 international marketplaces, partnering with AI researchers to optimize user engagement through A/B testing, resulting in 20% CTR increase and executive approval for global expansion

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, partnering with UX, engineering, and data science teams to enhance back-end logic and front-end UX/UI, increasing web navigation CSAT score (NPS) by 20% QoQ for 10M+ monthly users
gap_handling: The Staff-level seniority gap is real but bridgeable in the cover letter — emphasize the end-to-end ownership of Samsung.com's search roadmap (not just execution), your direct executive alignment experience, and frame the Amazon and Kolon tenures as startup-adjacent fast-moving environments. Avoid underselling scope: you owned search for a 10M+ MAU platform with global expansion decisions, which maps well to Faire's scale narrative.

title: Product Manager, API Growth
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, A/B testing discipline, and experience shipping ML-powered features at Samsung and Amazon are strong signals of competence for this role. However, the role is explicitly focused on developer-platform / API growth — a B2B/PLG funnel archetype — and Riley's portfolio is weighted toward consumer e-commerce discovery and search, with only one B2B platform role (Amazon seller tools) that wasn't growth-focused. The gap in dedicated developer-platform or API growth product experience (onboarding funnels, console UX, usage-based billing, self-serve upgrade paths) is real and is likely to be the primary screen filter.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML and growth fundamentals, but a meaningful gap in developer-platform / API-product experience means this is a competitive stretch requiring deliberate resume tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving growth through ML-powered search, discovery, and personalization products at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon — shipping at scale for 10M+ users and 2M+ B2B sellers. Proven track record leading full-funnel experimentation, activation optimization, and self-serve workflows that directly map to API platform growth levers including onboarding, conversion, and monetization.
top_bullets: Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — led self-serve workflow enhancements that saved $1.4M in operational cost and improved Ease-of-Use score by 380bps.

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users.
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% through model optimization and iterative A/B testing.
gap_handling: The core gap is developer-platform / API growth experience. To address this: (1) In your cover letter, explicitly bridge your Amazon B2B seller-tools work to developer-platform thinking — self-serve workflows, activation funnels, and structured data pipelines are directly analogous. (2) Reframe your AEO pipeline and recommendation API bullets to foreground the API/integration surface, not just the outcome. (3) If you have any side projects, personal API usage, or opinions on Claude's developer onboarding, weave that into your narrative to demonstrate genuine platform-side empathy.

title: Principal, Product Manager - AI / LLM
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product experience — spanning hybrid search, conversational AI, LLM pipelines, and RAG-adjacent work (AEO pipelines, recommendation APIs) — maps closely to the role's core focus on Copilot backend services and AI orchestration. The conversational AI chat experience at Samsung and the AEO metadata pipeline directly speak to the RAG and agent orchestration requirements. However, the role is explicitly titled "Principal PM" (a level Riley marks as "Avoid") and leans heavily into developer-platform and backend infrastructure domains (API platforms, SID error logs, B2B developer tooling) that differ from Riley's consumer/e-commerce discovery background. The 8+ years requirement is met, but the B2B developer/platform PM archetype is a meaningful gap.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/LLM signal matches well, but the Principal title exceeds Riley's target seniority and the developer-platform/B2B backend focus is a meaningful archetype stretch from consumer e-commerce discovery.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI chat experiences and hybrid search pipelines to AEO metadata automation — that drive measurable engagement at scale. Proven track record orchestrating cross-functional AI product delivery across backend data pipelines, contextual personalization, and intelligent user assistance for audiences of 10M+ users.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users

Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata (product attributes, inventory, pricing) from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%
gap_handling: The Principal title and B2B developer-platform focus are the two honest gaps here. For the title, Riley can lean into scope (10M+ users, multi-market launches, cross-org influence) to justify Principal-level impact without claiming the title directly. For the developer/B2B gap, the Amazon experience (2M+ B2B sellers, ML-driven listing pipelines, multi-modal classification) is the strongest bridge — lead with it when positioning for this role, and frame Samsung's internal tooling and AEO pipeline work as developer-adjacent backend product ownership.

The Samsung and Kolon work demonstrates real consumer-scale AI personalization with measurable outcomes, and the AEO pipeline shows fluency with structured data and retrieval architecture. The stretch is seniority: this is a Staff PM role, and while Riley's target includes Staff as a stretch, Airbnb's CS Platform framing (platform product, multi-surface infrastructure, cross-org alignment at Staff level) skews toward a more seasoned platform-builder — Riley's experience is more product-facing than infrastructure-platform-facing. No customer support / CS domain experience is also a gap the JD implicitly prizes.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI personalization and search signals, but this is a Staff-level platform infrastructure role in the CS domain — one level above Riley's target and outside her core domain; worth applying with tailored positioning.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping AI-powered personalization and search systems at consumer scale — including hybrid search, LLM-driven pipelines, and recommendation platforms serving 10M+ monthly users. Acknowledge in networking conversations that this is a stretch role, but lead with the breadth of AI personalization signals owned end-to-end.

title: Senior Product Manager
score_rationale: Riley's AI/search and e-commerce discovery background maps reasonably well onto the Help Center's "discovery-led" redesign and AI-powered self-service angle, and 9 years of experience easily clears the 5+ year bar. However, the core of the role — customer support platform strategy, help-center knowledge-base redesign, CustOps agent tooling, and deflection-rate metrics — sits notably outside Riley's primary track record in product search, ranking, and e-commerce discovery. The B2B context is secondary for Riley (Amazon seller tools), and there is no direct evidence of owning support/help surfaces or agent-facing internal tools. The NYC hybrid requirement vs. Riley's Bellevue base is also a meaningful location friction.
recommendation_reason: Solid AI and growth credentials partially match, but the role is primarily a customer-support platform redesign — a domain gap that requires deliberate tailoring to bridge.
suggested_summary: AI-native Senior Product Manager with 9+ years building discovery-led, AI-powered user experiences — including conversational AI chat, LLM-driven pipelines, and personalized discovery surfaces — at Samsung, Amazon, and Kolon. top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — to support product discovery and comparison for 10M+ monthly users.
Defined on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, increasing web navigation CSAT (NPS) by 20% QoQ through phased A/B-tested rollout across key funnels.

title: Staff Product Manager, Lyft AI Platform & Marketplace Applications
score_rationale: Riley's AI/ML product background, SQL/Tableau tooling, and experimentation chops are genuinely relevant to Lyft's AI Platform org — and the AEO pipeline + conversational AI bullets show she can work close to the LLM stack. However, the role is explicitly Staff PM, one level above her stated target (Senior/PM III), and the core ask is an internal AI platform / agentic-systems product, which is a meaningful archetype shift from Riley's consumer-facing search & discovery work. The job also stresses multi-agent orchestration, build-vs-buy vendor management, and CXO-level platform evangelism — areas that are thin or absent on the current resume.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML foundation and measurable impact qualify Riley for a serious look, but the Staff-level seniority stretch plus platform/agentic-AI archetype gap make this a competitive application that requires deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9 years of experience building and shipping AI-powered systems — from hybrid search and LLM-driven pipelines to conversational AI and recommendation platforms — at scale for millions of users. Proven track record partnering with ML engineers and data scientists to define roadmaps, instrument KPIs, and drive measurable business outcomes across consumer and B2B products.
top_bullets: Defined requirements and led delivery of an automated AEO strategy and pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks — demonstrating end-to-end ownership of an AI-powered platform capability.

Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience, from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch, in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering — directly relevant to Lyft's agentic AI platform mandate.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45% — evidence of close technical collaboration with scientists and rigorous KPI ownership.
gap_handling: The biggest gap is platform/internal-tooling PM experience and explicit agentic-systems knowledge. Address this in cover letter and interviews by reframing the AEO pipeline and conversational AI work as platform-building (cross-team enablement, adoption metrics, evangelist role). If you have any exposure to multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen) or vendor evaluation, add it under Familiar Skills. Consider briefly noting your MS in Business Analytics as grounding for the data-visualization and KPI instrumentation requirements Lyft calls out explicitly.

title: Product Manager, Monetization
location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI product credentials, deep e-commerce growth experience, and proven A/B testing / funnel optimization skills that map to parts of this role. However, the core ask is Monetization PM — specifically pricing & packaging, free-to-paid subscription conversion, and upgrade funnels for a SaaS/consumer AI product — an archetype Riley hasn't explicitly held. The Kolon Growth role comes closest (AOV, retention, onboarding conversion), but it's e-commerce transactional, not subscription monetization. Riley's Samsung and Amazon experience is heavier on search/discovery and B2B tooling, not revenue-growth funnels. The experience gap in pure monetization (pricing strategy, paywall design, subscription billing) is the key risk area.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/ML domain fit and proven growth metrics, but lacks explicit subscription monetization and pricing/packaging ownership that Anthropic is specifically hiring for.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years driving revenue-impacting growth across AI-powered consumer and e-commerce products — including search, discovery, and personalization at Samsung.com (10M+ monthly users) and ML-driven conversion optimization on a $2B+ GMV platform. Proven track record of shipping data-driven A/B experiments, funnel optimizations, and recommendation systems that directly grow engagement, conversion, and AOV — now focused on applying that toolkit to AI-native monetization at scale.
top_bullets: Restructured new customer benefit package and revamped onboarding workflow, increasing first purchase rate by 30%; designed segmentation-driven CRM marketing program boosting 1-year retention by 7% and re-purchase rate by 15%
Built ML-driven personalized recommendation systems across the conversion funnel (PDP, Cart, Checkout), increasing AOV by 25% ($148 to $185) through model optimization and iterative A/B testing

Defined the on-site search product vision and roadmap for Samsung.com, planning and leading a phased rollout with A/B testing across key funnels, increasing NPS by 20% QoQ and improving engagement for 10M+ monthly users

If you have any experience touching pricing pages, plan tiers, or billing flows — even informally — surface it explicitly. A short cover letter paragraph positioning your Samsung e-commerce conversion + AI product depth as uniquely suited for Claude.ai's consumer monetization will help bridge the gap.

location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials, a proven track record shipping search, discovery, and conversational AI at scale, and 9 years of PM experience that comfortably clears the 8-year bar. The hybrid SF/NYC in-office requirement (25%+) is manageable from Bellevue but adds friction, and the $385K–$460K comp signals this is a very senior, high-bar hire where the founder-pedigree filter is real.
recommendation_reason: Strong AI/consumer domain fit and solid credentials, but the "former founder" and 0-to-1 product invention requirement is a real gap that makes this a competitive stretch without startup experience.

Automated product listing workflows for 2M+ B2B sellers by launching an ML-driven auto-fill system, reducing manual listing time by 80% — partnering with science and engineering to design multi-modal classification and data-mapping pipelines across 20+ categories

The "AI-pilled" signal is important — reference specific AI tools you use daily (Cursor, Claude, etc.) to show cultural fit with what they're looking for.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
location: Bellevue, Washington; Chicago, Illinois; New York, New York; Washington, DC
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — shipping LLM pipelines, conversational AI, and search systems — which covers the "AI-adjacent" half of this role well. However, the core requirement is deep Identity & Access Management expertise (OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, Zero Trust, agent authentication/authorization), a domain Riley has no evident background in. This is a developer/security-platform PM role, not a consumer AI or e-commerce discovery role, so the archetype fit is meaningful but partial. suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years shipping LLM-powered systems — from conversational AI and hybrid search to structured data pipelines and recommendation APIs — at Samsung and Amazon scale. Experienced in defining technical protocols, partnering with engineers on system design, and launching "zero-to-one" AI products for millions of users; eager to apply this foundation to securing the next generation of AI agent identities at Okta.

gap_handling: Be upfront in your cover letter: acknowledge you're coming from the AI product side rather than the IAM/security side, and position yourself as the "AI-native PM who understands agentic systems from the inside." Reference your multi-agent workflow familiarity and API design experience explicitly. Consider quickly studying OAuth2/OIDC flows and Zero Trust basics (Auth0 docs, Okta developer blog) so you can speak credibly to the protocol layer in an interview. If you get a screen, lean hard into the AEO pipeline (AI agent traffic) and conversational AI work — those are the closest signals to "building for agents."

title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Automation
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, search ranking, recommendation systems — that directly map to Okta's "AI-native experiences" mandate. However, this is a Principal PM role requiring 10+ years, and Riley has 9 years, creating a slight seniority mismatch; more critically, the domain is enterprise identity/IAM and internal productivity tooling (IT, HR, Finance, GTM workflows), which is meaningfully different from Riley's consumer e-commerce and search expertise. The "agentic systems," "enterprise compliance," and "responsible AI" nice-to-haves are also largely absent from Riley's background, and the role is clearly a stretch above Riley's target seniority band (Senior PM / PM III).
recommendation_reason: Solid AI/ML product evidence makes Riley competitive, but the enterprise identity domain and Principal-level seniority bar are notable gaps requiring deliberate tailoring.
suggested_summary: AI Product Manager with 9+ years of experience shipping LLM-powered pipelines, conversational AI, and intelligent automation at scale — from hybrid search systems serving 10M+ users to production ML models driving measurable enterprise outcomes. Proven track record taking AI products from 0→1 to broad adoption, defining rigorous evaluation frameworks, and partnering cross-functionally to translate complex AI capabilities into simple, high-impact user experiences.
top_bullets: Led end-to-end delivery of on-site conversational AI chat experience — from scope definition and conversation flow design to production launch — in partnership with marketing, UX, and engineering to support product discovery and comparison for users.

Defined an automated AEO strategy and LLM-driven pipeline to extract and structure catalog metadata from PIM into schema markup, boosting AI agent traffic by 8x and increasing conversion by 40% within 12 weeks.

Partnered with ML engineers to improve search ranking using behavioral signals and catalog metadata, defining relevance metrics and experiment success criteria; improved precision@3 by 30% and 1st-result CTR by 45%.
gap_handling: Okta's domain is enterprise identity (IAM), with the role focused on AI for internal workflows (IT, HR, Finance, GTM) — not consumer e-commerce. In the resume and cover letter, lean hard into the platform/infrastructure angle: the AEO pipeline, recommendation API, and conversational AI work are your best proxies for "AI-native enterprise product" experience. Acknowledge the enterprise context gap indirectly by emphasizing cross-functional stakeholder leadership, 0→1 delivery, and scale. For the seniority gap, position your Samsung PM III title and scope (10M+ users, international expansion, executive alignment) as Principal-equivalent impact even if the title doesn't match exactly.

title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience
score_rationale: Riley has strong PM fundamentals — cross-functional leadership, data-driven experimentation, roadmap ownership, and 9 years of experience — that map well to Asana's stated SPM expectations. However, the core domain (B2B work management / enterprise SaaS productivity tooling) is a meaningful departure from Riley's AI search, e-commerce discovery, and consumer personalization background. recommendation_reason: Solid PM credentials and cross-functional track record qualify Riley, but domain fit (enterprise work management SaaS) is a genuine stretch from search/e-commerce; tailoring required to bridge the gap.

gap_handling: The biggest gap is enterprise work management / productivity SaaS domain experience — Asana builds task/project tooling for enterprise teams, which is structurally different from e-commerce search. In your cover letter and resume summary, lean into the Amazon SPM role (B2B platform, complex data models, 2M+ sellers) as your closest analog, and frame your Samsung work as large-scale, cross-functional, enterprise-grade product delivery. Avoid leading with consumer e-commerce or AI search framing; instead emphasize systems thinking, roadmap strategy, and enterprise UX instincts.

title: Senior Product Manager, Okta for AI Agents
score_rationale: Riley has strong AI/ML product credentials — LLM pipelines, conversational AI, agentic workflows, and multi-agent signals — that are directionally relevant.
